name: Bay Scallop Gougere
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before serving, prepare the gougère dough: Heat the butter and milk in a medium saucepan until boiling. Remove from heat. Add the flour and stir until the mixture is smooth and cleans the side of the pan. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the grated gruyere cheese, grainy mustard, salt, and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly over low heat, just until the cheese melts.
2. Butter a 15 x 10 baking sheet. Spread the dough evenly over the entire sheet, building up the sides and leaving just a thin layer of dough in the middle. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
3. The next day, preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Remove the gougère dough from the refrigerator. Spread the scallop mixture evenly over the dough. Sprinkle the grated gruyere cheese evenly over all. Bake until puffed and golden brown, about 40-45 minutes.
4. Let cool several minutes before cutting into bite-sized squares and serving with cocktails.
